title: iberis hispidula
intro: Iberis hispidula, also known as the Candytuft or Rock Candy, is a low-growing, perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its delicate, white or pink flowers and silvery-green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Iberis hispidula include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and provide relief from skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Iberis hispidula can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used to support immune function, calm digestive issues, and promote overall well-being.
